rulings.Case Title: NEW HORIZON TRUST ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 29, 2024 line 1. RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER to Amended PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION is SUSTAINED with leave to amend to address in good faith issues raised in the demurrer. All amendments shall be in bold font. Plaintiff's untimely opposition filed at 5:34 p.m. on Friday October 18, 2024 is stricken. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: WANDA ELSTON VS. JOHN STEWART COMPANY, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Christopher Weinberger, Esq.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 29, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANT JOHN STEWART COMPANY, AKA THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY, NICK NIXON, MCCORMACK & BARRQN Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Christopher Weinberger, Esq. is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is DENIED as to the punitive damages alleged in paragraphs 61, 65, 74, 98, 100, 115, and Number 9 on Page 20 of the complaint. The motion is GRANTED with 10-day leave to amend as to the treble damages claims alleged in paragraph 117 and Number 6 on Page 20 of the complaint. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: KIRA PAPINEAU VS. GLASS PROPERTIES, L.P., A DELAWARE LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 29, 2024 line 4. DEFENDANT GLASS PROPERTIES, L.P., VIKING SERVICES CORPORATION DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR. The parties are ordered to meet and confer in good faith. Parties are to meet and confer in-person or over the phone on any mutually agreeable day between October 29, 2024 and November 8, 2024. If parties cannot agree on a date and time, the parties must appear for an in-person meet and confer conference on November 7, 2024 at 2:00 p.m. in Department 501. Upon meaningful meet and confer conference parties to file a joint declaration attesting to good faith participation in a meaningful meet and confer conference. If unresolved issues remain, Defendant may re-file a motion to strike no later than November 15, 2024. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
